# Developer Instructions for arb4j

## CRITICAL: SWIG-Generated Files

The following Java source files are **auto-generated by SWIG** from `.i` interface files in `native/`:

- `src/main/java/arb/Real.java` ← `native/Real.i`
- `src/main/java/arb/Integer.java` ← `native/Integer.i` (if present, otherwise `native/arb.i`)
- `src/main/java/arb/Complex.java` ← `native/Complex.i`
- `src/main/java/arb/Fraction.java` ← `native/Fraction.i`
- `src/main/java/arb/AlgebraicNumber.java` ← `native/AlgebraicNumber.i`
- `src/main/java/arb/arblib.java` ← SWIG-generated wrapper
- `src/main/java/arb/arblibJNI.java` ← SWIG-generated JNI bridge

**Any changes to these files MUST also be made in the corresponding `.i` file in `native/`.** Changes made only to the generated `.java` file will be lost on the next SWIG regeneration.

When fixing a bug in one of these files, always:
1. Edit the `.i` file in `native/`
2. Edit the generated `.java` file in `src/main/java/arb/` (to keep them in sync without requiring a SWIG rebuild)
3. Verify both files match

## Known Bug Patterns

### Arithmetic Self-Aliasing (res == this)

Many arithmetic methods follow the pattern `op(X a, int bits, Y res)` where `res` is reused as scratch space before the operation. For example:

```java
public Real sub(Fraction a, int bits, Real res) {
    return sub(res.set(a), bits, res);  // BUG if res == this
}
```

When `res` and `this` are the same object, `res.set(a)` destroys `this` before the subtraction, producing `a - a = 0`. This is triggered by delegation chains like `Integer.sub(Fraction, int, Real)` → `result.set(this).sub(operand, prec)` where the callee's `this` IS `result`.

See [#911](https://github.com/crowlogic/arb4j/issues/911) for a full audit of this pattern.

## Build & Test

```bash
export LANG=en_US.UTF-8 LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-25-openjdk-amd64

# Compile
mvn compile

# Run a specific test
mvn test -Dtest="TestClass#testMethod"

# Run with debug flags (flags go via -DargLine, NOT in pom.xml)
mvn test -Dtest="TestClass#testMethod" \
  -DargLine="-Darb4j.saveClasses=true -Darb4j.decompileClasses=true -Darb4j.traceNodes=true -Darb4j.trace=true -Dlogback.configurationFile=logback-debug.xml"
```

See `DEBUGGING.md` for full details on debug flags and workflows.

## Native Library

`libarblib.so` in the project root is the pre-built native library. **Do not delete it.**

## Git

- Push directly to master. Do not create branches or PRs.
- Never delete or re-clone the workspace. Use `git pull` to update.
- Git identity: Name "Stephen Crowley", Email "stephencrowley214@gmail.com"
- Push via `gh api` (blob/tree/commit/ref update), not `git push` (auth doesn't work).

## Code Style

- Never use `instanceof` in a series of if/case/switch.
- Never return null for unimplemented functionality — throw an exception.
- Never generate stub bodies in abstract classes for what should be abstract method declarations.
- Use "declared variable", not "formal variable" / "formal parameter".
- Debug flags go via `-DargLine="..."` on the command line, never in pom.xml.
